title: How health coverage affects your 2022 federal income tax return
meta-title: How health coverage affects your 2022 federal income tax return
meta-description: You must file tax return for 2022 if you are enrolled in a Health Insurance Marketplace® plan. Learn how to maximize health care tax credit & get highest return.
title-short: How health coverage affects your 2022 federal income tax return
page_name: How health coverage affects your 2022 federal income tax return
excerpt: You must file tax return for 2022 if you are enrolled in a Health Insurance Marketplace® plan. Learn how to maximize health care tax credit & get highest return.
content:
If you haven’t filed your 2022 taxes, or filed but didn’t “reconcile” your premium tax credit, use the information on these pages. Some links may point to IRS information about filing this year’s taxes, so read any IRS pages carefully.

Get information about filing taxes for this year.

You should’ve already filed your 2022 taxes, but if you haven’t, you should do so immediately. When you file your taxes for the 2022 tax filing year:

  • You’ll provide more information when you file your 2022 federal income tax return.
  • You may have to complete one or two new tax forms.
  • You may have to use our tax tool to find 2022 Silver premiums to complete your tax return.

Your 2022 health coverage status

If you haven’t filed 2022 taxes, select the type of health coverage you had in 2022 to get next steps:

  • Marketplace plan with the premium tax credit
  • Marketplace plan without the premium tax credit
  • Job-based health coverage
  • Other health coverage
  • No health coverage
